original_abstract: This study outlines a community service initiative at Pondok Pesantren Nurul Amanah Bangkalan designed to foster economic independence and entrepreneurial character among students using the Asset-Based Community Development (ABCD) framework. By focusing on 10th and 11th-grade students, the program integrated theoretical instruction on Islamic business ethics (mu’amalah) with practical experience within the pesantren’s existing business ecosystem, including ventures in culinary arts, bottled water production, mushroom cultivation, and aquaculture. Results indicate a significant improvement in student engagement and understanding, with many participants demonstrating a new ability to navigate the practical challenges of business management. By leveraging local assets and religious foundations, the program successfully empowered students with sustainable professional skills while reinforcing the social and spiritual values essential to a sharia-compliant economy. Ultimately, this initiative transformed the pesantren’s business units into vital learning laboratories, providing a replicable model for developing self-reliant, character-driven entrepreneurs in a religious educational setting.
humanized_abstract: Title: Developing Characteristic Entrepreneurs Through Service Activity in Pondok Pesantren Nurul Amanah Bangkalan: Case Study at STMIK BENGKALAN On 13-15 September 2021, Ustadz Shalahuddin conducted a Service activity in a Pondok Pesantren called Nurul Amanah Bangkalan. The activity intended to strengthen the students to become innovative and able to make the resource in the environment into an entrepreneurial economic product, using the Asset-Based Community Development (ABCD) concept. The Service activity was directed towards the students of class 3SMA and 4SMA at the Pondok Pesantren Nurul Amanah Bangkalan consisting of 69 people. The material is enriched with knowledge of Islamic business ethics (mu’amalah). Besides, in addition to theoretical knowledge, the students will also learn how to apply it into business through being inserted to businesses around the Pondok Pesantren, including food business, bottled mineral water and mushrooms as well as fish farming. The results of service activities have shown that the students become more involved, are able to understand the material better and also increase their skills in managing business. After undergoing this program, students who participate in the service activity were later able to apply the concept of being a entrepreneurs after applying it, using the local resources and their deep-rooted Islamic values, so as to create more appropriate skills to reach the target of having economic and sharia-based economy. The campus businesses after getting service at Universitas Bengkalan are used as the alternatives of learning shops for the students with whom are believed able to develop innovative and character-based entrepreneurship.
